commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Corn. In times of scarcity. See Amos viii. 7.

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh He that withholds grain, the people shall curse him: but blessing shall be upon the head of him that sells it. It is well said by Solomon, “He that hides corn shall be cursed among the people. ” To hide corn is to retain with oneself the words of sacred preaching. Among the people any such one is cursed, since, for the fault of mere silence, he is condemned in the punishment of the many whom he could have corrected.

commentaries.author: Shenoute the Archimandrite
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh He that withholds grain, the people shall curse him: but blessing shall be upon the head of him that sells it. Shenoute the Archimandrite It is written, “Men speak evil of him who withholds wheat. ” One who withholds is without mercy. He does not gather for the sake of mercy. For, unless he gathers, with what can he bestow mercy. Is it not also true that men bless the person who expends and gives, as it is written here and there in the pages of sacred Scripture. On Language.
